steps.title: Read the sentence's logic
steps.content: Hedgehog tenrecs and true hedgehogs are not closely related, yet they share traits (spines, pointed snouts, small size) that they independently evolved — a classic case of convergent traits shared between two groups.

steps.title: Predict the missing word
steps.content: The blank needs a noun that means "shared traits" and pairs grammatically with "true hedgehogs." The phrase should read "share basic [shared features] with true hedgehogs."

steps.title: Check each choice for fit and preposition
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'examples of': would mean tenrecs share examples OF true hedgehogs, which is nonsensical — tenrecs aren't examples of hedgehogs. Wrong meaning. ✗
  • Choice B — 'concerns about': "concerns" (worries) makes no sense for biological traits like spines and snouts. Wrong word category. ✗
  • Choice C — 'indications of': would mean the traits are signs OF true hedgehogs, which misrepresents the relationship — tenrecs aren't pointing to hedgehogs, they parallel them. Wrong meaning. ✗
  • Choice D — 'similarities with': precisely names the shared traits between unrelated groups and takes the correct preposition "with." ✔
That matches choice D — D is correct.
